data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Instagram utilizes a microservices architecture to efficiently scale and manage its platform.
data.technical_spec: Services are independently deployable, loosely coupled, and organized around business capabilities.

data.title: Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: Leverages cloud hosting and scalable storage solutions to handle large volumes of data and user requests.
data.technical_spec: Primary use of AWS and other cloud platforms for scalable and high-availability architecture.

data.title: Real-time Data Processing
data.description: Facilitates real-time updates and interactions within the app, essential for social media engagement.
data.technical_spec: Uses streaming platforms like Apache Kafka for real-time data processing.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Utilizes a CDN for faster load times and efficient content delivery globally.
data.technical_spec: Employs multiple CDN providers to optimize user access across different regions.

data.title: Database Management
data.description: Employs a combination of SQL and NoSQL databases for various data needs.
data.technical_spec: Uses solutions like PostgreSQL and Cassandra for data storage and retrieval.
